title: Ps I Love You

jokes.text: Just for tonight, how about you let your buns down?
jokes.setup: Will agrees to go to Lakers game with Lindsey after she mentions perks
jokes.punchline: Sexual double entendre on 'let your hair down' idiom
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that fits Will's character, but relies on predictable sexual humor

jokes.text: Geoffrey, you look great today. Have you done something different with your hair? / Yes. I stopped pulling it out.
jokes.setup: Will's obvious flattery about Geoffrey's appearance
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's deadpan admission about stress-induced hair pulling
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Geoffrey's dry wit and Will's manipulation perfectly characterized

jokes.text: Marvelous. With what I'm paid... I should be able to afford one by the end of the day.
jokes.setup: Will selling $5 candy bars
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's sarcastic comment about his low wages
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Geoffrey's position in the household hierarchy

jokes.text: You poor, pathetic gigolo. How could any self-respecting man sell himself so cheap?
jokes.setup: Carlton is criticizing someone's behavior
jokes.punchline: The pompous, judgmental language coming from Carlton
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton pompousness with adult vocabulary. The 'gigolo' accusation is perfectly pretentious for his character.

jokes.text: Like I don't hear enough of that from my mother.
jokes.setup: Carlton calling Will a 'pathetic gigolo'
jokes.punchline: Will's self-deprecating reference to maternal criticism
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment but fairly predictable self-deprecation

jokes.text: I was talking to Will. / Of course. Why would anyone talk to me? I am but a poor, lowly servant.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey thinks Carlton is insulting him
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's over-dramatic self-pity when corrected
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Geoffrey's theatrical tendencies

jokes.text: Hey, besides, you know what they say about guys with big ears? / They can fly?
jokes.setup: Will setting up what seems like a sexual innuendo about big ears
jokes.punchline: Carlton's innocent Dumbo reference
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that perfectly captures Carlton's innocence vs Will's street knowledge

jokes.text: He's probably afraid he'll crack the concrete.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Uncle Phil not wanting to run
jokes.punchline: Will's fat joke about Phil's weight
jokes.explanation: Lazy fat joke with minimal setup or creativity

jokes.text: What'd he do? Have the halls widened?
jokes.setup: Phil's emotional story about Judge Robertson helping him through law school
jokes.punchline: Will's inappropriate fat joke about Phil needing wider halls
jokes.explanation: Another lazy fat joke that undermines Phil's emotional moment

jokes.text: I think I should have never said nothing about them halls.
jokes.setup: Phil's angry reaction to Will's hall-widening joke
jokes.punchline: Will's sheepish regret at inappropriate timing
jokes.explanation: Decent callback showing Will's awareness of his poor timing

jokes.text: What am I, psychic?
jokes.setup: Family asking weather girl Hilary about tomorrow's weather
jokes.punchline: Hilary's oblivious response about not being psychic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment showing her complete disconnect from her job responsibilities

jokes.text: I was born beautiful. What are you selling?
jokes.setup: Will complimenting Hilary's looks
jokes.punchline: Hilary's narcissistic dismissal and immediate suspicion
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - perfectly captures Hilary's vanity and cynicism

jokes.text: It's Ben.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey formally announcing Lindsey, then being overly proper about propriety
jokes.punchline: Flat correction when Lindsey calls him by wrong name
jokes.explanation: Nice deadpan moment showing Geoffrey's resignation to being misidentified

jokes.text: Right this way, money... Honey. I'm sorry.
jokes.setup: Will being polite to his guest Lindsey
jokes.punchline: Freudian slip revealing his true motivation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's mercenary mindset slipping through

jokes.text: Well, you know, nights do that sometimes.
jokes.setup: Lindsey saying she didn't want their magical night to end
jokes.punchline: Will's matter-of-fact response about nights ending
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response showing Will's detachment from romantic sentiment

jokes.text: I have obligations. I have commitments. I have a gold watch.
jokes.setup: Will making excuses to avoid spending time with Lindsey
jokes.punchline: Listing material possession alongside life responsibilities
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation that shows Will's materialistic priorities

jokes.text: Yo, G, here's the whipped cream you wanted for dinner. / Who said it was for dinner?
jokes.setup: Will delivering whipped cream to Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's suggestive response implying other uses
jokes.explanation: Mild sexual innuendo that's more suggestive than clever

jokes.text: This gold model was handcrafted in Switzerland by the finest of craftsmen. Keeps time accurate to within three seconds a year.
jokes.setup: Will asking Carlton for the time
jokes.punchline: Carlton's pretentious description of his expensive watch
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's pretentiousness but goes on too long

jokes.text: Love the Casio.
jokes.setup: Carlton's elaborate description of his expensive Swiss timepiece
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically identifying it as a cheap Casio
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Carlton's pretentiousness with economical sarcasm

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Carlton, I couldn't hear you. My damn watch was just ticking so loud.
jokes.setup: Carlton calling Will names while Will has the expensive watch
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically attributing his not listening to the loud ticking
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Will enjoying his material advantage over Carlton

jokes.text: I mean, take Moses... when he was asked to part the Red Sea, did he say, 'No, y'all build a dam'?
jokes.setup: Will justifying accepting gifts as being 'the chosen one'
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to Moses with modern slang twist
jokes.explanation: Clever biblical comparison with Will's street language creating good character humor

jokes.text: Here comes the judge. Here comes the judge. Order in the court! Here comes the judge.
jokes.setup: Judge Robertson arriving for dinner
jokes.punchline: Hilary doing exaggerated TV-style judge entrance routine
jokes.explanation: Fairly obvious TV reference joke that goes on too long

jokes.text: It wouldn't kill you to crack a smile, you know.
jokes.setup: Family's lack of enthusiasm for Hilary's judge routine
jokes.punchline: Hilary's defensive response to their stone faces
jokes.explanation: Decent reaction beat showing Hilary's ego but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Looks like you haven't stepped on the scales of justice in quite some time.
jokes.setup: Judge Robertson greeting the overweight Philip
jokes.punchline: Pun combining legal scales with weight scales
jokes.explanation: Clever legal pun that introduces Judge Robertson's inappropriate but witty nature

jokes.text: I'm laughing at something that happened yesterday. I'm done now.
jokes.setup: Judge Robertson not laughing at his own joke, then suddenly laughing
jokes.punchline: Explaining his delayed laugh is from yesterday
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd character moment establishing Judge Robertson's mental state

jokes.text: Ain't nothing wrong with his memory.
jokes.setup: Judge correctly identifying Carlton despite seeming confused
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic assessment of the judge's memory
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Will's street smart observation

jokes.text: There may be snow on the roof, but there's still fire in the furnace.
jokes.setup: Judge Robertson defending his mental faculties
jokes.punchline: Colorful metaphor about being old but still sharp
jokes.explanation: Standard old-timer saying that fits the character but isn't particularly original

jokes.text: You've aged.
jokes.setup: Judge Robertson mixing up Vivian and Hilary
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment after realizing his mistake
jokes.explanation: Perfectly blunt and inappropriate - great character moment with excellent economy

jokes.text: And what the hell is that you're wearing?
jokes.setup: Continued awkward interaction with Vivian
jokes.punchline: Judge inappropriately criticizing her outfit
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor establishing judge's inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: Benson.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey serving dinner
jokes.punchline: Judge calling Geoffrey by wrong name (TV show reference)
jokes.explanation: TV reference that works for the character's confusion but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: I didn't know you were really a butler.
jokes.setup: Judge calling Geoffrey 'Benson'
jokes.punchline: Judge's confusion between TV show and reality
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment about the judge's disconnect from reality

jokes.text: But, Aunt Viv, how come he keep calling you Weezie?
jokes.setup: Judge Robertson's continued confusion
jokes.punchline: Will noticing judge calling Vivian 'Weezie' (Jeffersons reference)
jokes.explanation: Another TV reference showing judge's confusion but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I asked him to pass the salt. He told me to go long. / He was joking, Vivian. / Philip, he broke a window.
jokes.setup: Vivian complaining about judge's behavior at dinner
jokes.punchline: Escalating from football joke to actual property damage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the absurd extent of judge's inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: Daddy, he said the only choice a woman should have... is with or without the lights on. / See, now that's disgusting. The lights should be off.
jokes.setup: Hilary reporting judge's sexist comment
jokes.punchline: Will's equally inappropriate response about lights
jokes.explanation: Decent setup/punchline but relies on sexual humor without much cleverness

jokes.text: That's ridiculous. What if she's serving coleslaw at a picnic?
jokes.setup: Judge's sexist comment about women on benches
jokes.punchline: Carlton's innocent literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection showing Carlton's innocent nature - perfect character comedy

jokes.text: Point taken. You have the makings of a fine judge.
jokes.setup: Carlton's innocent coleslaw response
jokes.punchline: Judge's sincere approval of Carlton's literal thinking
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that shows judge's declining judgment while praising Carlton

jokes.text: You see, the prosecutor was a babe... so after I decided in her favor, she decided in mine.
jokes.setup: Judge explaining his recent legal decision
jokes.punchline: Revealing he made judicial decision based on sexual quid pro quo
jokes.explanation: Strong cringe humor showing judge's corruption and inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: Good, maybe you can remember where I parked.
jokes.setup: Philip offering to walk judge to his car
jokes.punchline: Judge admitting he's forgotten where he parked
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing judge's mental decline in practical terms

jokes.text: Wow, a motorcycle jacket with Malcolm on it. I can ride and be righteous at the same time.
jokes.setup: Lindsey giving Will a motorcycle jacket
jokes.punchline: Will's excitement about combining style with political activism
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's street consciousness mixed with materialism

jokes.text: Yeah, right, like you wouldn't take it.
jokes.setup: Will accepting expensive gifts despite knowing it's wrong
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall to address audience's judgment
jokes.explanation: Great fourth-wall break that's character-specific and addresses audience directly

jokes.text: I was careful when I drove it through the house. / I mean where did it come from? / The Harley factory.
jokes.setup: Vivian asking about motorcycle on patio
jokes.punchline: Will's literal response to question about origin
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Will's evasiveness with literal answer

jokes.text: Now you know what you need to do, don't you? / Yeah, put out.
jokes.setup: Vivian asking Will what he should do about accepting gifts
jokes.punchline: Will's inappropriate sexual response
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection but relies on easy sexual humor

jokes.text: If I keep the bike, I'm a pimp. If I give it back, I'm a damn fool. Well, pimp it is.
jokes.setup: Will weighing his moral options
jokes.punchline: Choosing the morally questionable but materially beneficial option
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's honest self-assessment and moral flexibility

jokes.text: Forget it, honey. At $5 a pop, I'm not sharing.
jokes.setup: Vivian asking for chocolate
jokes.punchline: Philip's miserly response about expensive candy
jokes.explanation: Decent callback showing Philip's cheapness with the overpriced candy

jokes.text: Jesse Jackson?
jokes.setup: Vivian describing ideal judge candidate
jokes.punchline: Philip's misunderstanding of who she's describing
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection showing Philip's obtuseness about Vivian's hint

jokes.text: Get your hands off my chocolate, or you're a dead man.
jokes.setup: Philip being romantic with Vivian
jokes.punchline: Sudden threat over chocolate interrupting tender moment
jokes.explanation: Good tonal shift showing Philip's priorities shifting from romance to chocolate

jokes.text: No, I mean, that those women didn't shave under their arms.
jokes.setup: Looking at old photos from anti-war rally
jokes.punchline: Hilary focusing on grooming instead of historical significance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - missing important history for superficial observation

jokes.text: That's a relief, 'cause I was about to wonder... why Uncle Phil was holding that dude's hand.
jokes.setup: Will asking about skinny person in old photo
jokes.punchline: Relief it's Phil, not wondering about hand-holding
jokes.explanation: Decent observation joke about Phil's weight change over time

jokes.text: Well, big guy, may I suggest using flash cards with really big pictures?
jokes.setup: Phil wondering how to tell judge he's incompetent
jokes.punchline: Will's suggestion treating judge like a child
jokes.explanation: Good practical suggestion that treats judge's mental decline literally

jokes.text: If he's the man I think he is, he'll probably get lost on his way to the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Vivian saying judge will understand if he's the man Phil thinks
jokes.punchline: Will's realistic assessment of judge's current capabilities
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Phil's idealism and Will's realism about judge

jokes.text: For the last time, sir... I am not Benson.
jokes.setup: Judge calling Geoffrey 'Benson' again
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's frustrated correction
jokes.explanation: Running gag payoff showing Geoffrey's mounting frustration

jokes.text: Not only has she aged, she has a beer belly. / That's my wife and she's pregnant. / Then she should lay off the beer.
jokes.setup: Judge commenting on Vivian's appearance
jokes.punchline: Judge doubling down after learning she's pregnant
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation showing judge's complete lack of social awareness

jokes.text: Make the check out to cash. In fact, why don't you just give me the cash? Or maybe that ring.
jokes.setup: Judge thinking Phil wants to make campaign contribution
jokes.punchline: Escalating demands for immediate payment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing judge's corruption and desperation

jokes.text: Well, where did you have them last?
jokes.setup: Phil saying he can't find the words
jokes.punchline: Judge treating idiom literally
jokes.explanation: Excellent literal interpretation showing judge's mental disconnect

jokes.text: I'll be frank. / Okay, and who am I?
jokes.setup: Phil using common expression
jokes.punchline: Judge taking it as literal name change
jokes.explanation: Classic literal interpretation gag that fits judge's mental state

jokes.text: I don't have to wear pants, and besides, there's no one to run against me.
jokes.setup: Judge explaining why he won't retire
jokes.punchline: Including not wearing pants as job perk
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing judge's warped priorities and judicial privilege

jokes.text: And I can see myself in your head.
jokes.setup: Judge confronting Phil about running against him
jokes.punchline: Bizarre non-sequitur about seeing reflection in Phil's bald head
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd line that captures judge's stream-of-consciousness confusion

jokes.text: Wow. Takes a licking and keeps on ticking.
jokes.setup: Judge Robertson leaving after confrontation
jokes.punchline: Will's Timex slogan reference about judge's resilience
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference that fits Will's observational style

jokes.text: No wonder the garage door opener didn't work. It took forever to pry it open.
jokes.setup: Judge appearing at wrong house
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning breaking into garage
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor showing judge's complete disconnect from reality

jokes.text: Women. They've been a problem ever since they got the right to vote.
jokes.setup: Will mentioning woman problems
jokes.punchline: Judge's sexist historical perspective
jokes.explanation: Character-consistent sexism that reinforces judge's inappropriate worldview

jokes.text: What else? I married her.
jokes.setup: Judge describing girl who bought him gifts
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact admission of marrying for money
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery showing judge's transactional view of relationships

jokes.text: But the truth is, I dumped her after law school.
jokes.setup: Will expecting romantic happy ending
jokes.punchline: Judge revealing he used and dumped her
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that subverts expectation and shows judge's callousness

jokes.text: I got the money, the house, the car, the boat, and she got to keep the kids.
jokes.setup: Judge saying they split everything down the middle
jokes.punchline: Revealing unfair division and abandoning children
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation showing judge's complete moral bankruptcy

jokes.text: Possession is nine-tenths of the law, my boy.
jokes.setup: Will asking about keeping gifts after breakup
jokes.punchline: Judge using legal maxim to justify theft
jokes.explanation: Good character moment using judge's legal knowledge for immoral advice

jokes.text: Princess Leia! May the force be with you.
jokes.setup: Lindsey appearing in Star Wars costume
jokes.punchline: Will's Star Wars greeting mixing characters
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag with predictable pop culture reference

jokes.text: As if I didn't know, right?
jokes.setup: Lindsey asking what he's doing there when she shows up uninvited
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic response to her accusatory tone
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response to Lindsey's ridiculous question. Shows Will's growing awareness of the dynamic.

jokes.text: You ever think about going into politics?
jokes.setup: Lindsey's ruthless social manipulation
jokes.punchline: Judge recognizing political talent
jokes.explanation: Good observation linking ruthlessness to political success

jokes.text: You certainly weren't cheap. Cost me a fortune.
jokes.setup: Will saying he feels cheap
jokes.punchline: Lindsey coldly calculating her investment
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Lindsey's complete transactional view

jokes.text: You take this ring back, 'cause my mother don't scream at me like that.
jokes.setup: Will returning gifts after being yelled at
jokes.punchline: Comparing Lindsey unfavorably to his mother
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's standards and family respect

jokes.text: Nice meeting you, young lady. Be sure to rock the vote.
jokes.setup: Judge leaving after witnessing breakup
jokes.punchline: Encouraging Lindsey's political future with '90s slogan
jokes.explanation: Callback to politics comment with period-appropriate reference

jokes.text: I really loved that watch.
jokes.setup: Will reflecting on saying goodbye being hard
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's talking about the watch, not Lindsey
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Will's materialistic priorities over relationships

tmdb_overview: While Philip entertains rude, senile Judge Robertson, Will accepts expensive gifts from Lindsey Simon, a rich classmate who wants to buy his affection.
